title: Electronic trade processing system and method
abstract: In one implementation, a computer-implemented method, executed on a computing device and configured to effectuate a trading platform, includes: receiving a matched order concerning a first party and a second party; and effectuating the processing of the matched order including: sending via a requesting entity one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between a first custodial account associated with the first party and a second custodial account associated with the second party.
claims:
Claims What Is Claimed Is: 1. A computer-implemented method, executed on a computing device and configured to effectuate a trading platform, the computer-implemented method comprising: receiving a matched order concerning a first party and a second party; and effectuating the processing of the matched order including: sending via a requesting entity one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between a first custodial account associated with the first party and a second custodial account associated with the second party. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein effectuating the processing of the matched order further includes: seeking higher-level approval to confirm that the requesting entity has the authority required to send the one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between the first custodial account associated with the first party and the second custodial account associated with the second party. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the matched order concerns a bearer financial asset. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 3 wherein the bearer financial asset includes one or more of: a cryptocurrency; a fiat currency; and 

 a physical asset. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a fiat currency owned by the second party. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a precious metal owned by the second party. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a first cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a second cryptocurrency owned by the second party. 8. A computer program product residing on a computer readable medium having a plurality of instructions stored thereon which, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to perform operations comprising: receiving a matched order concerning a first party and a second party; and effectuating the processing of the matched order including: sending via a requesting entity one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between a first custodial account associated with the first party and a second custodial account associated with the second party. 9. The computer program product of claim 8 wherein effectuating the processing of the matched order further includes: 

 seeking higher-level approval to confirm that the requesting entity has the authority required to send the one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between the first custodial account associated with the first party and the second custodial account associated with the second party. 10. The computer program product of claim 8 wherein the matched order concerns a bearer financial asset. 11. The computer program product of claim 10 wherein the bearer financial asset includes one or more of: a cryptocurrency; a fiat currency; and a physical asset. 12. The computer program product of claim 8 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a fiat currency owned by the second party. 13. The computer program product of claim 8 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a precious metal owned by the second party. 14. The computer program product of claim 8 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a first cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a second cryptocurrency owned by the second party. 15. A computing system including a processor and memory configured to perform 

 operations comprising: receiving a matched order concerning a first party and a second party; and effectuating the processing of the matched order including: sending via a requesting entity one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between a first custodial account associated with the first party and a second custodial account associated with the second party. 16. The computing system of claim 15 wherein effectuating the processing of the matched order further includes: seeking higher-level approval to confirm that the requesting entity has the authority required to send the one or more messages to atomically effectuate transferring assets between the first custodial account associated with the first party and the second custodial account associated with the second party. 17. The computing system of claim 15 wherein the matched order concerns a bearer financial asset. 18. The computing system of claim 17 wherein the bearer financial asset includes one or more of: a cryptocurrency; a fiat currency; and a physical asset. 19. The computing system of claim 15 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a fiat currency owned by the second party. 20. The computing system of claim 15 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a precious metal owned by the second party. 21. The computing system of claim 15 wherein the matched order concerns a quantity of a first cryptocurrency owned by the first party and a quantity of a second cryptocurrency owned by the second party.
